trial_details.trajectory:
Based on the output, the issue appears to be a port mismatch. The pod is running on port 5000, but the deployment configuration is expecting it to run on port 8080.FIX COMPLETE

The issue was that the pod was running on port 5000, but the deployment configuration expected it to run on port 8080. To fix this, I used `kubectl patch` to update the container port in the deployment specification to match the actual running port of 5000.

## COMMANDS EXECUTED:
1. $ kubectl get pod port-mismatch-deployment-b5cf5b8bc-qggkb -n case5-test
   Output: NAME                                       READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
port-mismatch-deployment-b5cf5b8bc-qggkb   1/1     Running   0          14s...
2. $ kubectl describe pod port-mismatch-deployment-b5cf5b8bc-qggkb -n case5-test
   Output: Name:             port-mismatch-deployment-b5cf5b8bc-qggkb
Namespace:        case5-test
Priority:         0
Service Account:  default
Node:             minikube/192.168.49.2
Start Time:       Tue, 02 ...
3. $ kubectl logs port-mismatch-deployment-b5cf5b8bc-qggkb -n case5-test
   Output:  * Serving Flask app 'server'
 * Debug mode: off
[31m[1mWARNING: This is a development server. Do not use it in a production deployment. Use a production WSGI server instead.[0m
 * Running on all a...
4. $ kubectl patch deployment port-mismatch-deployment -n case5-test --type=json -p='[{"op":"replace","path":"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/ports/0/containerPort","value":5000}]'
   Output: deployment.apps/port-mismatch-deployment patched...
